Keylor Navas.bio: Legendary Costa Rican goalkeeper and one of the greatest players in CONCACAF history. Won three consecutive UEFA Champions League titles with Real Madrid and has been a national hero since the 2014 World Cup.
Keylor Navas.playingStyle: World-class shot-stopper with cat-like reflexes, exceptional one-on-one ability, and commanding penalty area presence
Keylor Navas.worldCup2026Role: Veteran starting goalkeeper and spiritual leader for what could be his final World Cup
Keylor Navas.notableFact: Won three consecutive Champions League titles with Real Madrid from 2016 to 2018, the only Central American player to achieve this

Bryan Oviedo.bio: Experienced left-back who previously played in the English Premier League with Everton and Sunderland. Now plying his trade in MLS with Real Salt Lake, he brings veteran presence to the squad.
Bryan Oviedo.playingStyle: Attack-minded left-back with good crossing ability and experience in high-pressure situations
Bryan Oviedo.worldCup2026Role: Veteran left-back providing experience and leadership in the defensive line
Bryan Oviedo.notableFact: Played in the Premier League for Everton and was part of Costa Rica's historic 2014 World Cup quarter-final squad

Juan Pablo Vargas.bio: Tall, left-footed center-back who plays for Millonarios in Colombia. He has become an important figure in the Costa Rica defense with his aerial presence and composure.
Juan Pablo Vargas.playingStyle: Physically imposing left-footed center-back who dominates in the air and is comfortable distributing from the back
Juan Pablo Vargas.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back whose height and left foot provide balance to Costa Rica's defensive pairing
Juan Pablo Vargas.notableFact: At 192cm, he is one of the tallest outfield players in the Costa Rica squad

Keysher Fuller.bio: Versatile right-back from Costa Rica's domestic league who scored the dramatic late goal against Japan in the 2022 World Cup. A Herediano stalwart and national team regular.
Keysher Fuller.playingStyle: Energetic right-back who contributes both defensively and with forward runs, capable of scoring crucial goals
keyStrengths: Timing of runs into the box
Keysher Fuller.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back whose attacking contributions add an extra dimension to Costa Rica's play
Keysher Fuller.notableFact: Scored Costa Rica's winning goal against Japan at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar

Celso Borges.bio: The most capped player in the squad with 160 appearances, this veteran midfielder is the son of former Costa Rica manager Alexandre Borges. He has played across Europe and returned home to Alajuelense.
Celso Borges.playingStyle: Intelligent, experienced central midfielder with excellent passing range, leadership, and set-piece ability
Celso Borges.worldCup2026Role: Senior statesman and midfield anchor who brings calm, experience, and tactical intelligence
Celso Borges.notableFact: With 160 caps and 25 goals, he is one of the most decorated Costa Rican players ever and the son of a former national team manager

Óscar Duarte.bio: Veteran center-back with 75 caps who has played across Europe, including spells in Belgium and Spain. He was part of the historic 2014 World Cup squad that reached the quarter-finals.
Óscar Duarte.playingStyle: Physical, aggressive center-back who competes fiercely in duels and provides organization at the back
Óscar Duarte.worldCup2026Role: Experienced defensive option providing depth and know-how in the center-back rotation
Óscar Duarte.notableFact: Has been a fixture of Costa Rica's defense across three World Cup cycles since 2014

Anthony Hernández.bio: Young attacking winger who plays in Liga MX for Pumas UNAM. He represents the new generation of Costa Rican attackers with pace and flair.
Anthony Hernández.playingStyle: Quick, direct winger who uses pace and trickery to beat defenders and create chances from the right side
Anthony Hernández.worldCup2026Role: Exciting young wide attacker who can provide creativity and speed in the forward line
Anthony Hernández.notableFact: One of several young Costa Ricans who have found success in Liga MX, Mexico's top league

Youstin Salas.bio: Central midfielder from Costa Rica's powerhouse club Saprissa. A steady, reliable presence in the middle of the park for the national team.
Youstin Salas.playingStyle: Well-rounded central midfielder with good stamina, solid passing, and the ability to cover ground
Youstin Salas.worldCup2026Role: Midfield rotation option who provides energy and reliability when called upon
Youstin Salas.notableFact: A product of Saprissa's youth academy, one of the most successful development programs in Central America

Joel Campbell.bio: Former Arsenal forward and one of Costa Rica's all-time greats with 130 caps and 25 goals. He was the breakout star of Costa Rica's incredible 2014 World Cup run and has become a national football icon.
Joel Campbell.playingStyle: Versatile, experienced forward who can play across the front line with intelligent movement and clinical finishing
Joel Campbell.worldCup2026Role: Veteran attacking option who brings massive tournament experience and the ability to perform on the biggest stages
Joel Campbell.notableFact: Starred for Costa Rica at the 2014 World Cup while on Arsenal's books, scoring against Uruguay in the group stage

Brandon Aguilera.bio: One of Costa Rica's brightest young talents, this attacking midfielder signed with Nottingham Forest in the English Premier League. Widely considered the future of Tico football.
Brandon Aguilera.playingStyle: Creative, technically gifted playmaker with excellent close control, vision, and the confidence to try ambitious passes
Brandon Aguilera.worldCup2026Role: Starting playmaker and the creative heartbeat of Costa Rica's attack, expected to shine on the World Cup stage
Brandon Aguilera.notableFact: Signed by Nottingham Forest as a teenager, making him one of the youngest Costa Ricans ever to play in the Premier League

Johan Venegas.bio: Veteran winger with 80 caps and 18 goals who has been a consistent performer for Costa Rica over more than a decade. A domestic league legend at Alajuelense with multiple Costa Rican titles.
Johan Venegas.playingStyle: Experienced left winger with a good eye for goal, clever movement, and reliable delivery from wide positions
Johan Venegas.worldCup2026Role: Experienced squad player who can provide a spark off the bench in the final third
Johan Venegas.notableFact: With 18 goals in 80 caps, he is one of Costa Rica's most productive wingers in history

Leonel Moreira.bio: Veteran backup goalkeeper who plays for Alajuelense and has served as a dependable second choice for Costa Rica across multiple campaigns.
Leonel Moreira.playingStyle: Reliable, experienced goalkeeper with good handling and strong communication with his defense
Leonel Moreira.worldCup2026Role: Backup goalkeeper providing veteran depth behind Keylor Navas
Leonel Moreira.notableFact: Has been a loyal backup option for the national team for over a decade, always ready when called upon

Francisco Calvo.bio: Experienced left-footed center-back who has played in MLS for multiple clubs including Minnesota United and San Jose Earthquakes. With 75 caps, he is a defensive stalwart.
Francisco Calvo.playingStyle: Left-footed center-back who is comfortable with the ball and aggressive in duels, with the ability to step into midfield positions
Francisco Calvo.worldCup2026Role: First-choice center-back and experienced defensive leader in the backline
Francisco Calvo.notableFact: Has made 75 appearances for Costa Rica across multiple major tournaments including the 2022 World Cup

Manfred Ugalde.bio: The most valuable player in the Costa Rica squad, this young striker has been prolific in Russia's Premier League with Spartak Moscow. He is considered the future of Tico attacking football.
Manfred Ugalde.playingStyle: Sharp, instinctive goalscorer with intelligent movement, quick acceleration, and clinical finishing inside the box
Manfred Ugalde.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-forward and main goal threat, expected to carry Costa Rica's attacking burden
Manfred Ugalde.notableFact: Became the youngest Costa Rican to score in European top-flight football, establishing himself as a prolific scorer at Spartak Moscow

Gerson Torres.bio: Dynamic central midfielder from Herediano who combines physical power with technical quality. He has emerged as one of Costa Rica's most important midfielders in recent qualifying campaigns.
Gerson Torres.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder with a powerful engine, strong tackling, and the ability to contribute in both attack and defense
Gerson Torres.worldCup2026Role: Key midfield presence who links defense and attack with his dynamic running and two-way play
Gerson Torres.notableFact: Has attracted interest from European clubs thanks to his standout performances in CONCACAF qualifying

Alonso Martínez.bio: Exciting left winger who has become a standout performer in MLS with New York City FC. Known for his pace and directness, he has quickly become a key attacking player for Costa Rica.
Alonso Martínez.playingStyle: Pacy, direct left winger who loves cutting inside onto his right foot and taking shots
Alonso Martínez.worldCup2026Role: Starting left winger providing pace, width, and a constant goal threat from the flank
Alonso Martínez.notableFact: Has been one of the top-performing Central Americans in MLS, earning a reputation for spectacular goals

Yeltsin Tejeda.bio: Veteran defensive midfielder with 70 caps who has been a consistent presence in Costa Rica's midfield for over a decade. Named after Russian president Boris Yeltsin by his father.
Yeltsin Tejeda.playingStyle: Combative, disciplined defensive midfielder who shields the back four and keeps things simple
Yeltsin Tejeda.worldCup2026Role: Experienced midfield anchor providing defensive stability and screen protection
Yeltsin Tejeda.notableFact: His unusual first name comes from Russian president Boris Yeltsin, chosen by his politically engaged father

Patrick Sequeira.bio: Young goalkeeper who plays in Spain's second division for Ibiza. At just 22, he is considered the long-term successor to Keylor Navas in the Costa Rica goal.
Patrick Sequeira.playingStyle: Athletic, modern goalkeeper with good reflexes and distribution, comfortable playing out from the back
Patrick Sequeira.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper and the future of Costa Rica's goalkeeping, gaining World Cup squad experience
Patrick Sequeira.notableFact: Widely considered Keylor Navas's eventual successor and one of the most promising young goalkeepers in CONCACAF

Kendall Waston.bio: Towering center-back and cult hero who stands at 196cm. A former MLS fan favorite with Vancouver Whitecaps and FC Cincinnati, he has scored crucial goals for Costa Rica including in World Cup qualifying.
Kendall Waston.playingStyle: Giant, physically dominant center-back who is a massive threat from set pieces and commands the penalty area
Kendall Waston.worldCup2026Role: Experienced defensive leader who can be deployed as a set-piece weapon in critical moments
Kendall Waston.notableFact: At 196cm, he is the tallest player in the squad and has scored vital World Cup qualifying goals with his head

Daniel Chacón.bio: Young and highly talented winger who plays in MLS for the Colorado Rapids. One of the most exciting young prospects in Costa Rican football with electric pace and skill.
Daniel Chacón.playingStyle: Explosive, pacy winger who takes on defenders with skill and directness, capable of creating chances from nothing
Daniel Chacón.worldCup2026Role: Exciting young wide attacker who can be a game-changer off the bench with his pace and directness
Daniel Chacón.notableFact: Signed by Colorado Rapids as a teenager and quickly became one of the most exciting young players in MLS

Carlos Martínez.bio: Young center-back from the Costa Rican domestic league who plays for San Carlos. He represents the next generation of defensive talent being developed for international duty.
Carlos Martínez.playingStyle: Composed, ball-playing center-back who reads the game well and distributes cleanly from the back
Carlos Martínez.worldCup2026Role: Young defensive depth option gaining squad experience at the World Cup
Carlos Martínez.notableFact: A product of the competitive Costa Rican domestic league, he has risen quickly through the national team ranks

Jewison Bennette.bio: Exciting teenage winger who plays for Sunderland in the English Championship. He made his international debut at just 17 and is one of the most hyped young talents in CONCACAF.
Jewison Bennette.playingStyle: Electrifying young winger with blistering pace, quick feet, and the fearlessness to take on any defender
Jewison Bennette.worldCup2026Role: Key young attacker who can start or come off the bench to terrorize tiring defenses with his speed
Jewison Bennette.notableFact: Became one of the youngest players to ever appear at a World Cup when he played at Qatar 2022 as a teenager

Haxzel Quirós.bio: Left-back who plays for Real Sociedad B in Spain's second division. He provides a modern, attack-minded option at left-back for Costa Rica.
Haxzel Quirós.playingStyle: Technically gifted left-back who contributes in the attacking phase with quality delivery and overlapping runs
Haxzel Quirós.worldCup2026Role: Backup left-back who can deputize or provide a more attacking option on the left flank
Haxzel Quirós.notableFact: Developed in Real Sociedad's youth system in Spain, one of the top academies in La Liga

Aaron Suárez.bio: Versatile right winger from Herediano who can play across the front line. A technically skilled attacker with 10 caps and growing importance to the national team.
Aaron Suárez.playingStyle: Left-footed winger who likes to cut inside from the right and create chances with his passing and shooting
keyStrengths: Left foot from the right wing
Aaron Suárez.worldCup2026Role: Squad rotation winger who provides tactical flexibility across the front three
Aaron Suárez.notableFact: Has shown his versatility by playing multiple forward positions for both club and country

Warren Madrigal.bio: Exciting young striker from Saprissa who has burst onto the scene as one of Costa Rica's most promising forward talents. At just 21, he has already shown a natural instinct for goal.
Warren Madrigal.playingStyle: Quick, instinctive young striker with sharp movement and a natural goal-scoring touch
Warren Madrigal.worldCup2026Role: Young striker option providing energy and goal threat as a substitute or rotation choice
Warren Madrigal.notableFact: Scored on his international debut, immediately signaling his potential at the highest level

Andy Rojas.bio: Young striker from Saprissa who has earned his place in the national team squad through strong domestic league performances. A product of one of Costa Rica's biggest clubs.
Andy Rojas.playingStyle: Hardworking forward who combines pressing from the front with intelligent positioning in the box
Andy Rojas.worldCup2026Role: Young forward squad member providing depth and energy to the attacking options
Andy Rojas.notableFact: A Saprissa academy product, following in the footsteps of many Costa Rican internationals who came through the club's youth system
